title: ficus baileyana
intro: Ficus baileyana, also known as the Banyan Fig, is a species of fig tree native to Australia and New Guinea. The medicinal properties of Ficus baileyana include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Ficus baileyana is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in poultices to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
